hook: You already know how to count outcomes and compute simple fractions — the building blocks of probability. But contest problems push further, asking for the *expected* number of steps in a random process, the chance a dart lands in a shaded region, or the long-run behavior of a system that jumps between states. This unit gives you the precise tools to crack those problems confidently and efficiently.
concept: Every topic here is about quantifying uncertainty in clever ways — from counting favorable outcomes to modeling multi-step random processes — unified by the idea of *probability as a rigorous measure over structured sample spaces*.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to set up sample spaces carefully and apply counting techniques to compute exact probabilities in the tricky, non-routine scenarios that appear on AMC, AIME, and similar contests.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Expected Value and Linearity of Expectation
topic_roadmap.description: Master the powerful trick of breaking a complex random quantity into simple indicator variables so that expected value calculations — even over dependent events — become straightforward sums.
Apply area, length, and volume ratios to compute probabilities when outcomes are drawn continuously from a geometric region, turning contest problems into elegant geometry puzzles.

You will learn to model random processes where each step depends only on the current state, setting up and solving systems of equations to find hitting probabilities and expected times — a hallmark technique in harder olympiad and AIME problems.
